title: BSP GCTRL access-mode bit audit
observations: The BSP defines GCTRL access bits SDXC_MEMORY_ACCESS_DONE, SDXC_ACCESS_DONE_DIRECT, SDXC_ACCESS_BY_AHB, and SDXC_ACCESS_BY_DMA.
The BSP host init clears SDXC_ACCESS_DONE_DIRECT and writes REG_A12A=0.
The local diagnostic tree already clears SDXC_ACCESS_DONE_DIRECT during init and writes REG_A12A=0.
Before enabling A733 IDMAC, the local diagnostic tree clears SDXC_ACCESS_BY_AHB so the controller is in DMA access mode.
The failing logs already occur after that local A733 DMA-access transition.
reason: The BSP GCTRL access-mode clue is already implemented in the diagnostic branch. It does not explain why IDMAC reaches DESC_READ with DLBA set but does not consume descriptor word 0.
next: Do not reopen GCTRL access-mode bits unless new evidence shows a different A733-specific access bit outside REG_GCTRL.
